page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 architecture virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, with 4 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate moderate performance for basic operations but limitations under heavy multi-threaded loads. The single-core stress-ng performance is in the bottom 10%, and multi-core performance falls in the bottom 25%. However, memory bandwidth for read-only small blocks (398.82 GB/sec) and large blocks (40.7 GB/sec) registers in the middle 50%. Passmark CPU scores (8006.57) and database operations (2632.17) also achieve average ratings, while write-heavy memory bandwidth (23.27 GB/sec) and Redis SET operations (5.58 million operations/sec) are weaker. Small LLM inference (135M model) performs at an average tier, processing 402.78 tokens/sec for prompts and 157.76 tokens/sec for text generation.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory-to-CPU ratio, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and development or testing environments. The lack of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses requires external configuration for persistent storage and external networking.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM. With a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio, no bundled local storage, and no GPUs, this instance is designed for budget-conscious workloads. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities in memory read bandwidth (up to 398.82 GB/sec) and Passmark CPU tests (8006.57), though multi-core stress-ng and Redis SET operations fall into weaker percentiles. The shared CPU allocation makes it a cost-efficient option for non-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic database operations where sustained CPU performance is not critical.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering budget-friendly performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, designed for budget-friendly, light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server with balanced memory read speeds for lightweight development and basic web serving workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Average performance in memory read bandwidth and basic database operations
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
